title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new digital banking app they’ve both started using.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’ve finally switched to 'Nova-Bank'. The instant notifications for every transaction are so helpful. || Woman: [1]I found the automated budgeting categories to be quite inaccurate[/1], to be honest. It tagged my grocery shop as 'entertainment' for some reason. || Man: I see what you mean. It does take a while to 'train' the AI. || Woman: [2]What I really appreciated, though, was the ease of freezing my card via the app[/2]. I thought I’d lost my wallet yesterday and it was such a relief to do it instantly. || Man: I agree. The security features are definitely a step up from my old high-street bank. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a great tool for managing your money, as long as you don't mind manually correcting your spending tags every now and then.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’ve finally switched to 'Nova-Bank'. The instant notifications for every transaction are so helpful.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the automated budgeting categories to be quite inaccurate, to be honest. It tagged my grocery shop as 'entertainment' for some reason.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. It does take a while to 'train' the AI.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I really appreciated, though, was the ease of freezing my card via the app. I thought I’d lost my wallet yesterday and it was such a relief to do it instantly.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. The security features are definitely a step up from my old high-street bank.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a great bank, as long as you don't mind manually correcting your spending tags every now and then.

questions.question: What is the woman's main criticism of the banking app?

options.A: the high fees for international transfers
options.B: the poor quality of the automated categorization
options.C: the slow response time of the customer support

questions.explanation: She finds the budgeting categories 'inaccurate', noting groceries were tagged as 'entertainment' [1].

questions.question: Both speakers are impressed by the app's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'security features' and the ability to 'freeze the card' instantly [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a new corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiative.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, the company is going to plant a tree for every ten emails we send. What do you think? || Woman: [3]I suspect the board is just using this as a distraction from our poor environmental record in manufacturing[/3]. || Man: It does feel a bit like 'greenwashing'. Why not just upgrade the factory equipment? || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that it’s actually encouraging people to send more unnecessary emails[/4] just to 'hit their tree target', which is completely counter-productive. || Man: I agree. It’s a bit of a logical mess. || Woman: Exactly. If they really wanted to make a difference, they’d focus on reducing our carbon footprint where it actually matters, rather than these small symbolic gestures.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, the company is going to plant a tree for every ten emails we send. What do you think?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the board is just using this as a distraction from our poor environmental record in manufacturing.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It does feel a bit like 'greenwashing'. Why not just upgrade the factory equipment?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that it’s actually encouraging people to send more unnecessary emails just to 'hit their tree target', which is completely counter-productive.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s a bit of a logical mess.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. If they really wanted to make a difference, they’d focus on reducing our carbon footprint where it actually matters, rather than these small symbolic gestures.

questions.question: The woman believes the new initiative was designed to

options.B: divert attention from larger ecological issues.
options.C: increase the speed of project delivery.

questions.explanation: She thinks it's a 'distraction from our poor environmental record in manufacturing' [3].

questions.question: What paradox do both speakers identify regarding the program?

options.A: It is too expensive for the results it achieves.
options.B: It promotes the behavior it should be discouraging.
options.C: It is unpopular despite the positive impact on the community.

questions.explanation: They agree it's 'counter-productive' because people send 'unnecessary emails' to meet targets [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a recently opened co-working space in the suburbs.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’ve been working from 'The Hive' this week. It’s a lot better than my kitchen table. || Woman: [5]I found the lack of private phone booths to be a bit of a problem[/5], to be honest. I didn't want the whole room hearing my client calls. || Man: I see what you mean. It can get a bit loud in the open-plan area. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate the quality of the on-site cafe, though[/6]. The coffee was better than most high-street chains. || Man: I agree. It makes staying there all day much more enjoyable. || Woman: Definitely. It’s a great place for creative work, as long as you don't have too many confidential meetings to attend.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’ve been working from 'The Hive' this week. It’s a lot better than my kitchen table.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the lack of private phone booths to be a bit of a problem, to be honest. I didn't want the whole room hearing my client calls.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. It can get a bit loud in the open-plan area.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate the quality of the on-site cafe, though. The coffee was better than most high-street chains.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It makes staying there all day much more enjoyable.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. It’s a great space, as long as you don't have too many confidential meetings to attend.

questions.question: What did the woman find annoying about the co-working space?

options.A: the high cost of the daily pass
options.B: the insufficient privacy for business calls
options.C: the poor quality of the internet connection

questions.explanation: She notes the 'lack of private phone booths' and doesn't want others to hear her calls [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the space's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'quality of the on-site cafe' and the coffee [6].
